violations.text: Raw animal foods (shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables, cheese) in downstairs refrigerator #2. Raw animal foods must be separated (below) from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Sauce (various containers) observed with temperatures 45 - 60 degrees F. Proper cooling reviewed. Voluntary disposal during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of cook, pan/containers of sauce placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Resource provided. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients were not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (tortillas) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Tortillas were voluntarily disposed of during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
